title_english: Lord, Where Shall I Find Thee?
overview: Written by the preeminent poet of the Spanish Golden Age, this piyut (liturgical poem) was composed for the morning service. It was inspired by the philosophical tension between God's "transcendence" (being far away and infinite) and His "immanence" (being close and personal). HaLevi wrote it to express the seeker's journey: searching for the Divine in the vastness of the cosmos only to discover that God is most present within the human heart. It remains a masterpiece of medieval Jewish Neo-Platonism.
poem_lines: Lord, where shall I find Thee?
High and hidden is Thy place!
And where shall I not find Thee?
With all my heart I called Thee,
And going out to meet Thee
I found Thee coming toward me!
In the wonders of Thy might
In the sanctuary, I beheld Thee.
The yoke of Thy kingdom is upon all.
Who shall not call to Thee?
Thou givest food to all in its time.
With all my heart I called Thee,
And going out to meet Thee
I found Thee coming toward me!
poem_hebrew_lines: כִּי אַתָּה נוֹתֵן אֹכֶל לַכֹּל בְּעִתּוֹ.
